test_suite.details: node test.js — 3616 passed, 0 failed. Covers internal anchors, tab structure, data-prose consistency, WIN detail, Phase A design checks, prediction panels.

internal_anchors.details: Covered by test.js (Section 3). No manual re-check needed — tests are authoritative and pass cleanly.

tab_structure.details: Covered by test.js Phase A design refresh tests. All pass.

external_links.details: External link check not performed this run (network access constrained). Previous run (2026-05-15) confirmed 9/9 data-source links reachable. No changes to prose link set detected since that run.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json=71 entries, 69 unique dome WIN numbers (2 paired sub-entries: WIN-057b, WIN-058b). llms.txt correctly states '71 WIN entries documenting all 69 dome WIN numbers (V51.1)'. uncounted-failures.json: 18 entries, all valid (id/dome_ref/dome_label/what_actually_happened present, no dupes, all FAIL-NNN format). Meta tags: description, og:title, og:description, ClaimReview LD+JSON all present. test.js covers verdict tallies and summary table counts.

win_detail_consistency.details: Covered by test.js. 3616 tests pass with no failures.

collisions.dome_prospective_claim: SAA Simultaneous Acceleration (PRED-R026) — in prospective section with id='win-058-kappa' (id mismatch)
collisions.our_coverage: WIN-057 = Two-Zone Disc Topology; WIN-057b = Global cavity re-pressurization (SAA synchronous decay). Both covered.

win_dome_alignment.details: Dome site still shows 69 confirmed WINs. WIN-057 has a documented number collision (appears in both prospective and confirmed sections with different claims). Our review correctly handles this via WIN-057 + WIN-057b. Similarly WIN-058 + WIN-058b. Alias cross-references (PROS-NNN → WIN-NNN) are correctly identified. No new uncovered WINs detected. No dome WINs removed or renumbered since last check.

build_reproducibility.details: node build.js html run from fresh clone. Only diff: build date in header (May 16 vs May 15 — auto-generated at build time). No data, structure, or content drift. Site was last published 2026-05-15; today's clone builds May 16 as expected. Not a real drift.

discoverability_infrastructure.details: llms.txt: present, contains funwithscience.net/dome-model-review URL, dome model named, key findings described, counts correct (71 WIN entries / 69 dome WINs). sitemap.xml: valid XML with urlset root, main page + llms.txt URLs present. robots.txt: Allow: / present, Sitemap directive present. All OK.

expansion_tracker.details: next_id=393 > max_id=392 — allocator is correct. Live and archive are fully disjoint. No orphaned EXP-NNN.json output files. No phantom tracker entries. No stale pending items. MODERATE: 2 live tracker items have terminal state (integrated=true, status=complete) and should have been moved to archive: EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 and EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1541. These are not collision risks (no allocation from live array currently), but they are predicate violations per PROP-022 phase 5. Note: 43 live items use 'baby-orphan-ISS-NNN' IDs (non-EXP namespace) — these are analyst-baby consolidation tracking items, correctly excluded from EXP ID continuity checks.

workspace_sync_skips.details: No direction-violation skips in last 24h. 39 mtime-guard skips (normal — git version is newer than workspace for historical applied-patches and category-proposals files). No action required.

curmudgeon_digest.details: Digest regenerated fresh (node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js --workspace . exit 0). 0 parse errors. 1 unprocessed: WIN-038.c9.json — within normal pipeline lag (decider's last run was 2026-05-15T21:14Z; WIN-038 re-verification is queued at qid 361).

section_queue_coupling.details: 0 section-new or category-proposal-writeup tracker items require queue coupling. OK.

progressive_disclosure.details: Covered by test.js. All 3616 tests pass.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: Scan of sections.json for hardcoded light-theme colors (#fff, #ffffff, #f8f9fa, etc.) in background: declarations returned 0 matches.

drift_audit.details: drift-audit.json was 91.9h old (threshold 72h). compute-drift-audit.js run successfully. 18 candidates for curmudgeon change-detection: 3 verdict-changed, 11 large-rewrite, 4 tldr-only. 30 pre-TLDR schema entries (expected backlog). Curmudgeon now has fresh candidate list.

workspace_only_files.details: All curmudgeon reviews and analyst expansions are committed to git. No at-risk files.

issues_found.description: 2 live expansion-tracker items have terminal state (integrated=true, status=complete) and should have been moved to archive by the verifier/decider integration writer. EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 and EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1541. Per PROP-022 phase 5, live items with these predicates should be in archive. Not a collision risk currently (next_id=393 is safe), but indicates the live→archive move failed for these two items.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Move both entries to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl and remove from live items[] array. Verify idempotent (guard if already in archive).
issues_found.tracked_under: ISS-1533 (partial match — ISS-1533 covers decider blocked-items scanner reading EXP tracker; the specific terminal-in-live predicate violation may need a new ISS)

issues_found.description: PAT stored in CLAUDE.md returns 403 on push to GitHub (write denied). Decider is using a fallback workspace PAT as workaround. Tracked under HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-AUTH-403-001 (filed 2026-05-11 by tinker). This has been ongoing for 5 days without operator resolution.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Operator should regenerate a new PAT with write:repo scope and update CLAUDE.md. Until fixed, decider push reliability depends on the workspace PAT remaining valid.

issues_found.description: Root partition at 93% after cleanup (742M free). Before this run's clone was removed it was at 99% (189M free). With /tmp/edit-clone at ~486MB and each agent clone adding 290-550MB, the partition can reach 100% if multiple clones accumulate. Per-session clone discipline must be maintained.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Operator-side: periodically verify /tmp only has one active *-clone directory. This integrity run cleaned its clone on exit (policy compliant). If disk climbs above 95% again, operator should inspect /tmp for stranded clones.

summary: Site health is WARN (no critical or major findings). All structural checks pass: test.js 3616/3616, build reproducibility clean (only auto-generated date differs), all discoverability files correct, no broken anchors, no orphaned EXP files, no parse errors in curmudgeon reviews, no direction-violation workspace-sync skips, and no hardcoded light-theme colors. Two moderate/minor infrastructure items: (1) two expansion-tracker live items carry terminal state and should be archived — not a collision risk but a predicate violation per PROP-022 phase 5; (2) the CLAUDE.md PAT has returned 403 on push for 5 days (decider is using fallback). Disk was at 99% before this run's clone cleanup; now 93% (742M free) — risk remains elevated. Drift audit was refreshed successfully (was 91.9h stale, now current with 18 candidates). Dome site alignment: still 69 confirmed WINs, WIN-057 number collision already correctly handled as WIN-057 + WIN-057b. No new dome WINs or renumbering detected. Priority for decider: archive EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 and EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1541 from live tracker; operator should regenerate CLAUDE.md PAT.
